current_suggestion.pitch: Your carnivore/low-carb channels (Saladino, Chaffee, Ekberg) share a broadly aligned ideological axis, while Renaissance Periodization argues from the conventional-science side — Brad Kearns sits in the underrepresented middle, coming from an endurance-athletic background and engaging seriously with both ancestral health and performance data, including critical takes on chronic cardio and zone-2 dogma. He frequently covers recovery, hormonal stress responses to overtraining, and longevity tradeoffs that your current lineup skips past. Format is a mix of solo deep-dives and long-form interviews, typically 45–90 minutes, with genuine nuance rather than tribal positioning.
